title: Takeshi Honda
wikitext:
'''Takeshi Honda''' (本田 雄) is an animator that worked on the [[Neon Genesis Evangelion]] TV series, [[The End of Evangelion]], and [[Rebuild of Evangelion]] as well as being credited with the mechanical designs of the [[Mass Production Evangelions]] that appeared in [[Episode 25']]. Honda did a lot of work involving the [[Evangelions]] in the movies and is credited with the mechanical animation director.

Takeshi Honda was born on March 12, 1968 in Ishikawa Prefecture. He dropped out of the Tokyo Design Academy to join [[Gainax]] as an art supervisor and character designer. Other projects that he has worked on include Nadia (animation director, key animation, ending animation), Gunbuster (animator), Millenium Actress (character design, animation director), and Ponyo on the Cliff by the Sea (key animation).

==External links==
*[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Takeshi_Honda_%28animator%29 Wikipedia Entry]
*[http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/people.php?id=1163 Anime News Network page]

html:
Takeshi Honda (本田 雄) is an animator that worked on the Neon Genesis Evangelion TV series, The End of Evangelion, and Rebuild of Evangelion as well as being credited with the mechanical designs of the Mass Production Evangelions that appeared in Episode 25'. Honda did a lot of work involving the Evangelions in the movies and is credited with the mechanical animation director.

Takeshi Honda was born on March 12, 1968 in Ishikawa Prefecture. He dropped out of the Tokyo Design Academy to join Gainax as an art supervisor and character designer. Other projects that he has worked on include Nadia (animation director, key animation, ending animation), Gunbuster (animator), Millenium Actress (character design, animation director), and Ponyo on the Cliff by the Sea (key animation).
